Artifact 2 docs/design/KEEL-104-dashboard-mockup.html: exists, 369 lines, valid HTML5 with DOCTYPE, self-contained (grep for cdn./googleapis/cloudflare/unpkg/jsdelivr/script-src/link-href all return no matches), no external CDN references. Table present with all 6 columns (Story ID 100px, Title auto, Scope 90px, Current Phase 160px, Status 130px, Idle Time 100px right-aligned). Four sample data rows: KEEL-104 IN PROGRESS phase 3, KEEL-102 IN PROGRESS phase 5, KEEL-103 HALTED, KEEL-101 COMPLETE. All 3 status badge colors verified in HTML against spec. Single permitted style block contains only @keyframes pulse — no other class-based styles. Empty state and error row documented as HTML comments. Footer shows timestamp + port. Meta refresh tag present. No executable claims to verify (UI Designer phase produces only design docs, not running code).

decisions: Inline CSS only (no CDN/Tailwind/Bootstrap): the server generates HTML as a string — external stylesheets would require serving additional routes or bundling, adding complexity that contradicts the zero-dependency constraint.
Single @keyframes pulse <style> block permitted: keyframe animations have no inline-style equivalent; this is the minimal exception to the inline-only rule and has no runtime dependencies.

Status badge colors match cmdReport exactly (not approximate): design consistency between CLI and browser matters more than a 'prettier' palette; engineers should never need to map between two color systems.
Idle Time column right-aligned with white-space: nowrap: numbers right-align naturally for comparison; nowrap prevents '1h 7m' splitting across lines on narrow columns.

Empty state uses Unicode emoji (📂) not an SVG icon: SVG would require additional markup and escaping in the template string; the emoji is a single character, renders at any size, and requires no external asset.

Error rows use #fef2f2 background (not a badge): an error is a data-quality signal on the row, not a status classification — using the same badge component would imply parity with HALTED/COMPLETE/IN PROGRESS, which is incorrect.

Phase column shows 'Phase N — Agent Name' (not just integer): the integer alone is meaningless to a user scanning the dashboard; the agent name anchors context without requiring hover or a separate legend.

Tier NORMAL (no auth/payments/security paths; diff is additive new module <100 lines changed in existing files). Schema validation: engine returned PASS — schema valid, artifacts exist, no AC drift. Artifact 1: docs/design/KEEL-104-design.md — confirmed on disk, opens correctly, references ADR-003, contains component diagram and full function-level spec for scripts/keel-dashboard.cjs. Artifact 2: .keel/memory/decisions/ADR-003-dashboard-architecture.md — confirmed on disk, status Accepted, date 2026-07-14. ADR content audit: options considered (inline vs new file), rationale for Option B chosen, security detail (127.0.0.1 loopback bind), EADDRINUSE handler, escHtml XSS requirement, lock-free state reading pattern consistent with ADR-001, PHASE_NAMES duplication rationale for AC-7 safety. Design integrity: no executable claims made by this phase (SA produces design artifacts only, no test-pass claims). Referenced symbols (scripts/keel-dashboard.cjs, bin/keel.js ROUTES, run() helper) are either new files or existing named targets verified via Glob/Read — no phantom references. AC-7 regression guard addressed structurally (new file, zero edits to keel-state.cjs) and explained in both ADR and design doc. No issues found.

decisions: Dashboard in new scripts/keel-dashboard.cjs (not inline in bin/keel.js): 300+ line HTML generator + HTTP lifecycle in the CLI dispatcher would conflate two concerns and make the module hard to test from a CJS harness.
Explicit loopback bind ('127.0.0.1'): security requirement — developer-tool server must not be reachable from LAN without explicit intent.
No shared constants with keel-state.cjs: PHASE_NAMES and SCOPES are duplicated to preserve the AC-7 zero-touch guarantee on keel-state.cjs.
escHtml() required on all manifest-sourced strings: cheap XSS defense even though manifests are written by the pipeline engine; defense in depth.
generateHTML is a pure function: enables unit testing of HTML output without an HTTP server; aligns with testability-first design.
Error StoryRows sorted last (not first): an operator scanning the dashboard sees healthy stories first; errors are a secondary concern that do not obscure normal pipeline state.
notes: auto-audited on gate PASS

decisions: Dashboard implemented as new scripts/keel-dashboard.cjs (CJS) per ADR-003 — 300+ line HTML generator + HTTP lifecycle isolated from bin/keel.js CLI dispatcher
main() called at bottom of module (entry-point pattern); module.exports exposes all functions for CJS test harness without starting the server separately
formatIdle called with Math.max(0, ms) to clamp negative clock-skew values — prevents '-1m 0s' display for future updated_at timestamps
PHASE_NAMES and SCOPES duplicated from keel-state.cjs — not shared, to preserve AC-7 zero-touch guarantee on keel-state.cjs
Error StoryRows sorted last (not first) — operator sees healthy stories first, errors are secondary; consistent with architect decision in 04-solution-architect.json

decisions: Chose Option B stub verification (file rename) — cleanest proof that all 70 tests require the module; avoids per-function stubbing for a module that calls main() at load time
Suppressed main() server startup via http.createServer monkey-patch before require() — avoids EADDRINUSE and stray server in test process without modifying production code
startDashboard() tested at unit level by intercepting http.createServer with a stub that fires the listen callback immediately and capturing process.stdout.write — avoids real port binding in CI
AC-7 regression guard implemented as a subprocess spawn of keel-state.cjs with a tmp fleet (same pattern as keel-state-describe.test.cjs) to validate the real command end-to-end
